mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC exceeds 0.02 when FEDFUNDS increases, contradicting discount rate compression theory.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or t-statistic of return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing firms to cut capital expenditures and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 50% of cases, or regime-split regression shows no significant negative coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 5.0% combined with persistent inflation above 200 index points forces aggressive portfolio de-risking, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of observations when both thresholds are breached, or conditional mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real personal consumption expenditures exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden drop in consumer demand, prompting earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances following a > 0.5% RSAFS decline, or event study shows no significant negative alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates inventory buildup and weak downstream demand, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-HOUST divergence signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to reach statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
